title: Traditional Greek Lamb Koftas with Keftedes
description: A classic Greek dish consisting of juicy lamb koftas served with crispy keftedes, perfect for a warm evening.

ingredients.name: Lamb mince
ingredients.notes: Choose a high-quality lamb mince for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Finely chop the onion to prevent it from overpowering the lamb

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic to release its flavors

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chop the parsley to add freshness to the koftas

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Use a fresh egg to bind the koftas together

ingredients.name: Greek seasoning
ingredients.notes: Add a mix of dried oregano, thyme, and rosemary for a traditional Greek flavor

ingredients.name: Flour
ingredients.notes: Use all-purpose flour to coat the keftedes before frying

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral oil with a high smoke point for frying the keftedes

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the lamb mince, chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, egg, and Greek seasoning. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the mixture with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Divide the lamb mixture into 8-10 portions and shape each portion into a small kofta.
Thread the koftas onto skewers, if using, and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the koftas are cooking, prepare the keftedes. In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
Dip each kofta into the flour mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry the keftedes for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and crispy.
Serve the lamb koftas with the crispy keftedes and a side of your favorite Greek salad or roasted vegetables.
